name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)
summary: DORA is an EU regulation establishing a comprehensive framework for digital operational resilience in the financial sector. It harmonizes ICT risk management, incident reporting, digital operational resilience testing, and third-party risk management rules across all EU financial entities including banks, insurers, investment firms, and their critical ICT service providers.
keyRequirements: ICT risk management framework with governance, identification, protection, detection, response, and recovery capabilities
Major ICT-related incident reporting to competent authorities within 4 hours (initial), 72 hours (intermediate), and 1 month (final)
Digital operational resilience testing including threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) every 3 years for significant entities
Third-party ICT risk management with mandatory contractual provisions for critical service providers
Oversight framework for critical ICT third-party service providers designated by European Supervisory Authorities
Information sharing arrangements on cyber threat intelligence between financial entities
penalties: Competent authorities may impose administrative penalties and remedial measures as defined by member state law. Critical ICT third-party service providers face periodic penalty payments of 1% of average daily worldwide turnover. Member states may impose criminal penalties for serious violations.
